It is identified with “Kuroda Shrine” in Haguri District, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. === Overview === *The founding period is unknown. Since {{jalink|Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842}}, father of Princes Oke and Woke, is said to have been killed by Emperor Yūryaku in {{jalink|Emperor Ankō|Q361010}} 3 ({{jalink|456|Q30432}}), it is presumed this shrine had already been founded before then. Kuroda Shrine was the formal name, but it was also called Komori Katte Shrine at that time. *It was long regarded as an important shrine. There are records of imperial offerings in the reigns of {{jalink|Empress Kōken|Q232955|Empress Shōtoku}} and {{jalink|Emperor Uda|Q349267}}, and in {{jalink|951|Q23654}} ({{jalink|Tenryaku|Q1346281}} 5), an envoy of {{jalink|Emperor Murakami|Q374015}} prayed there for recovery from illness. *In {{jalink|1582|Q6719}} ({{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 10), it was rebuilt by {{jalink|Sawai Katsushige|Q11553123}}, lord of {{jalink|Kuroda Castle (Owari Province)|Q11678587|Kuroda Castle}} and chief retainer of {{jalink|Oda Nobukatsu|Q3044815}}. *In the Edo period it was called “Kuroda Daimyōjin” and “Hakusan Shrine.” When it became gōsha for the northwestern Haguri District area in {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it was renamed Komori Katte Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Haraedo no Ōkami|Q11588504|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Yodobime-no-Mikoto}} (Kokūtsuhime-no-Mikoto) *:Child of {{jalink|Okinaga Sukune no Ō|Q11492327}} and elder (or younger) sister of {{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}}. === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Waniguchi gong (craft) - late Muromachi period * Image of Komori Kuroda Daimyōjin honji (painting) * Ninja sword (craft) - early Edo period * Bamboo spear (craft) - early Edo period === Transportation === *15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Kisogawa Station|Q871019}} on the {{jalink|Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152}}. *20 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Kuroda Station (Aichi)|Q6446282|Kuroda Station}} on the {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838}} of {{jalink|Nagoya Railroad|Q30850}}. *1 minute on foot from “Matsuyama-higashi” stop on the Kisogawa/Kitagata route of {{jalink|i-Bus|Q11223722}}. *5 minutes on foot from “{{jalink|Ichinomiya Municipal Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital|Q11352566|Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital}}” stop on the same i-Bus route. === Mikago Festival === A ritual held on lunar August 15. Images of Princes Oke and Woke are placed in {{jalink|kago|Q936754}} palanquins, taro is offered, and prayers are made for warding off misfortune and abundant harvest. This derives from the following tradition. *{{jalink|Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842}}, father of Oke and Woke, was a prince of {{jalink|Emperor Richū|Q329704}}. {{jalink|Emperor Ankō|Q361010}} (his nephew) intended to pass the throne to him, but in Ankō 3 ({{jalink|456|Q30432}}), Ankō was assassinated by {{jalink|Mayowa no Ōkimi|Q11582590}}. Prince Ōhatsuse (later {{jalink|Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235}}), who had long resented this, lured Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko to hunting grounds at Kayano in {{jalink|Ōmi Province|Q843882}} and shot him. *The brothers Oke (later {{jalink|Emperor Ninken|Q357266}}) and Woke (later {{jalink|Emperor Kenzō|Q360966}}), fearing further danger, fled the capital. It was the full-moon night of lunar August 15. Villagers treated them kindly with dishes of {{jalink|taro|Q227997}}. former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} | 創建 = Unknown | 別名 = Hakusan-sha
Kuroda Daimyōjin | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Komori
Katte Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Komori Katte Shrine''' (こもりかってじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kuroda, Kisogawa-chō|Q17210627}}, {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. It is identified with “Kuroda Shrine” in Haguri District, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. === Overview === *The founding period is unknown. Since {{jalink|Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842}}, father of Princes Oke and Woke, is said to have been killed by Emperor Yūryaku in {{jalink|Emperor Ankō|Q361010}} 3 ({{jalink|456|Q30432}}), it is presumed this shrine had already been founded before then. Kuroda Shrine was the formal name, but it was also called Komori Katte Shrine at that time. *It was long regarded as an important shrine. There are records of imperial offerings in the reigns of {{jalink|Empress Kōken|Q232955|Empress Shōtoku}} and {{jalink|Emperor Uda|Q349267}}, and in {{jalink|951|Q23654}} ({{jalink|Tenryaku|Q1346281}} 5), an envoy of {{jalink|Emperor Murakami|Q374015}} prayed there for recovery from illness. *In {{jalink|1582|Q6719}} ({{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 10), it was rebuilt by {{jalink|Sawai Katsushige|Q11553123}}, lord of {{jalink|Kuroda Castle (Owari Province)|Q11678587|Kuroda Castle}} and chief retainer of {{jalink|Oda Nobukatsu|Q3044815}}. *In the Edo period it was called “Kuroda Daimyōjin” and “Hakusan Shrine.” When it became gōsha for the northwestern Haguri District area in {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it was renamed Komori Katte Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Haraedo no Ōkami|Q11588504|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Yodobime-no-Mikoto}} (Kokūtsuhime-no-Mikoto) *:Child of {{jalink|Okinaga Sukune no Ō|Q11492327}} and elder (or younger) sister of {{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}}. === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Waniguchi gong (craft) - late Muromachi period * Image of Komori Kuroda Daimyōjin honji (painting) * Ninja sword (craft) - early Edo period * Bamboo spear (craft) - early Edo period === Transportation === *15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Kisogawa Station|Q871019}} on the {{jalink|Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152}}. *20 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Kuroda Station (Aichi)|Q6446282|Kuroda Station}} on the {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838}} of {{jalink|Nagoya Railroad|Q30850}}. *1 minute on foot from “Matsuyama-higashi” stop on the Kisogawa/Kitagata route of {{jalink|i-Bus|Q11223722}}. *5 minutes on foot from “{{jalink|Ichinomiya Municipal Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital|Q11352566|Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital}}” stop on the same i-Bus route. === Mikago Festival === A ritual held on lunar August 15. Images of Princes Oke and Woke are placed in {{jalink|kago|Q936754}} palanquins, taro is offered, and prayers are made for warding off misfortune and abundant harvest. This derives from the following tradition. *{{jalink|Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842}}, father of Oke and Woke, was a prince of {{jalink|Emperor Richū|Q329704}}. {{jalink|Emperor Ankō|Q361010}} (his nephew) intended to pass the throne to him, but in Ankō 3 ({{jalink|456|Q30432}}), Ankō was assassinated by {{jalink|Mayowa no Ōkimi|Q11582590}}. Prince Ōhatsuse (later {{jalink|Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235}}), who had long resented this, lured Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko to hunting grounds at Kayano in {{jalink|Ōmi Province|Q843882}} and shot him. *The brothers Oke (later {{jalink|Emperor Ninken|Q357266}}) and Woke (later {{jalink|Emperor Kenzō|Q360966}}), fearing further danger, fled the capital. On their way toward {{jalink|Masumida Shrine|Q705916}} in Owari, they passed through Mino and reached Kuroda Shrine. *They stopped their palanquin in the forest of Kuroda Shrine and spent the night inside. It was the full-moon night of lunar August 15. Villagers treated them kindly with dishes of {{jalink|taro|Q227997}}. In thanks, the princes offered dew from taro leaves to Kuroda Shrine, prayed for villagers’ happiness, and departed before dawn. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こもりかつてしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

(UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称 = Komori Katte Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Komorikatte shrine1.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}{{ill|lt=Kuroda, Kisogawa-chō|Q17210627|WD=Q17210627}} Ōkanhigashi Higashinokiri 11 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yodobime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 創建 = Unknown | 別名 = Hakusan-sha
Kuroda Daimyōjin | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Komori
Katte Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Komori Katte Shrine''' (こもりかってじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kuroda, Kisogawa-chō|Q17210627|WD=Q17210627}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is identified with “Kuroda Shrine” in Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. === Overview === *The founding period is unknown. Since {{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842}}, father of Princes Oke and Woke, is said to have been killed by Emperor Yūryaku in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010}} 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432}}), it is presumed this shrine had already been founded before then. Kuroda Shrine was the formal name, but it was also called Komori Katte Shrine at that time. *It was long regarded as an important shrine. There are records of imperial offerings in the reigns of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267}}, and in {{ill|lt=951|Q23654|WD=Q23654}} ({{ill|lt=Tenryaku|Q1346281|WD=Q1346281}} 5), an envoy of {{ill|lt=Emperor Murakami|Q374015|WD=Q374015}} prayed there for recovery from illness. *In {{ill|lt=1582|Q6719|WD=Q6719}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 10), it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Sawai Katsushige|Q11553123|WD=Q11553123}}, lord of {{ill|lt=Kuroda Castle (Owari Province)|Q11678587|WD=Q11678587|lt=Kuroda Castle}} and chief retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobukatsu|Q3044815|WD=Q3044815}}. *In the Edo period it was called “Kuroda Daimyōjin” and “Hakusan Shrine.” When it became gōsha for the northwestern Haguri District area in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it was renamed Komori Katte Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yodobime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} (Kokūtsuhime-no-Mikoto) *:Child of {{ill|lt=Okinaga Sukune no Ō|Q11492327|WD=Q11492327}} and elder (or younger) sister of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}}. === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Waniguchi gong (craft) - late Muromachi period * Image of Komori Kuroda Daimyōjin honji (painting) * Ninja sword (craft) - early Edo period * Bamboo spear (craft) - early Edo period === Transportation === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kisogawa Station|Q871019|WD=Q871019}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152}}. *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kuroda Station (Aichi)|Q6446282|WD=Q6446282|lt=Kuroda Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850}}. *1 minute on foot from “Matsuyama-higashi” stop on the Kisogawa/Kitagata route of {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|WD=Q11223722}}. *5 minutes on foot from “{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Municipal Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital|Q11352566|WD=Q11352566|lt=Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital}}” stop on the same i-Bus route. === Mikago Festival === A ritual held on lunar August 15. Images of Princes Oke and Woke are placed in {{ill|lt=kago|Q936754|WD=Q936754}} palanquins, taro is offered, and prayers are made for warding off misfortune and abundant harvest. This derives from the following tradition. *{{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842}}, father of Oke and Woke, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Richū|Q329704|WD=Q329704}}. {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010}} (his nephew) intended to pass the throne to him, but in Ankō 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432}}), Ankō was assassinated by {{ill|lt=Mayowa no Ōkimi|Q11582590|WD=Q11582590}}. Prince Ōhatsuse (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}}), who had long resented this, lured Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko to hunting grounds at Kayano in {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}} and shot him. *The brothers Oke (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninken|Q357266|WD=Q357266}}) and Woke (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Kenzō|Q360966|WD=Q360966}}), fearing further danger, fled the capital. On their way toward {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916}} in Owari, they passed through Mino and reached Kuroda Shrine. *They stopped their palanquin in the forest of Kuroda Shrine and spent the night inside. It was the full-moon night of lunar August 15. Villagers treated them kindly with dishes of {{ill|lt=taro|Q227997|WD=Q227997}}. In thanks, the princes offered dew from taro leaves to Kuroda Shrine, prayed for villagers’ happiness, and departed before dawn. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こもりかつてしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 創建 = Unknown | 別名 = Hakusan-sha
Kuroda Daimyōjin | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Komori
Katte Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Komori Katte Shrine''' (こもりかってじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kuroda, Kisogawa-chō|Q17210627|WD=Q17210627}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is identified with “Kuroda Shrine” in Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. === Overview === *The founding period is unknown. Since {{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842}}, father of Princes Oke and Woke, is said to have been killed by Emperor Yūryaku in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010}} 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432}}), it is presumed this shrine had already been founded before then. Kuroda Shrine was the formal name, but it was also called Komori Katte Shrine at that time. *It was long regarded as an important shrine. There are records of imperial offerings in the reigns of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267}}, and in {{ill|lt=951|Q23654|WD=Q23654}} ({{ill|lt=Tenryaku|Q1346281|WD=Q1346281}} 5), an envoy of {{ill|lt=Emperor Murakami|Q374015|WD=Q374015}} prayed there for recovery from illness. *In {{ill|lt=1582|Q6719|WD=Q6719}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 10), it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Sawai Katsushige|Q11553123|WD=Q11553123}}, lord of {{ill|lt=Kuroda Castle (Owari Province)|Q11678587|WD=Q11678587|lt=Kuroda Castle}} and chief retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobukatsu|Q3044815|WD=Q3044815}}. *In the Edo period it was called “Kuroda Daimyōjin” and “Hakusan Shrine.” When it became gōsha for the northwestern Haguri District area in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it was renamed Komori Katte Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yodobime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kokūtsuhime-no-Mikoto) *:Child of {{ill|lt=Okinaga Sukune no Ō|Q11492327|WD=Q11492327}} and elder (or younger) sister of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}}. === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Waniguchi gong (craft) - late Muromachi period * Image of Komori Kuroda Daimyōjin honji (painting) * Ninja sword (craft) - early Edo period * Bamboo spear (craft) - early Edo period === Transportation === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kisogawa Station|Q871019|WD=Q871019}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152}}. *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kuroda Station (Aichi)|Q6446282|WD=Q6446282|lt=Kuroda Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850}}. *1 minute on foot from “Matsuyama-higashi” stop on the Kisogawa/Kitagata route of {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|WD=Q11223722}}. *5 minutes on foot from “{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Municipal Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital|Q11352566|WD=Q11352566|lt=Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital}}” stop on the same i-Bus route. === Mikago Festival === A ritual held on lunar August 15. Images of Princes Oke and Woke are placed in {{ill|lt=kago|Q936754|WD=Q936754}} palanquins, taro is offered, and prayers are made for warding off misfortune and abundant harvest. This derives from the following tradition. *{{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842}}, father of Oke and Woke, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Richū|Q329704|WD=Q329704}}. {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010}} (his nephew) intended to pass the throne to him, but in Ankō 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432}}), Ankō was assassinated by {{ill|lt=Mayowa no Ōkimi|Q11582590|WD=Q11582590}}. Prince Ōhatsuse (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}}), who had long resented this, lured Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko to hunting grounds at Kayano in {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}} and shot him. *The brothers Oke (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninken|Q357266|WD=Q357266}}) and Woke (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Kenzō|Q360966|WD=Q360966}}), fearing further danger, fled the capital. On their way toward {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916}} in Owari, they passed through Mino and reached Kuroda Shrine. *They stopped their palanquin in the forest of Kuroda Shrine and spent the night inside. It was the full-moon night of lunar August 15. Villagers treated them kindly with dishes of {{ill|lt=taro|Q227997|WD=Q227997}}. In thanks, the princes offered dew from taro leaves to Kuroda Shrine, prayed for villagers’ happiness, and departed before dawn. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こもりかつてしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Since {{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842}}, father of Princes Oke and Woke, is said to have been killed by Emperor Yūryaku in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010}} 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432}}), it is presumed this shrine had already been founded before then. Kuroda Shrine was the formal name, but it was also called Komori Katte Shrine at that time. *It was long regarded as an important shrine. There are records of imperial offerings in the reigns of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267}}, and in {{ill|lt=951|Q23654|WD=Q23654}} ({{ill|lt=Tenryaku|Q1346281|WD=Q1346281}} 5), an envoy of {{ill|lt=Emperor Murakami|Q374015|WD=Q374015}} prayed there for recovery from illness. *In {{ill|lt=1582|Q6719|WD=Q6719}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 10), it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Sawai Katsushige|Q11553123|WD=Q11553123}}, lord of {{ill|lt=Kuroda Castle (Owari Province)|Q11678587|WD=Q11678587|lt=Kuroda Castle}} and chief retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobukatsu|Q3044815|WD=Q3044815}}. *In the Edo period it was called “Kuroda Daimyōjin” and “Hakusan Shrine.” When it became gōsha for the northwestern Haguri District area in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it was renamed Komori Katte Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yodobime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kokūtsuhime-no-Mikoto) *:Child of {{ill|lt=Okinaga Sukune no Ō|Q11492327|WD=Q11492327}} and elder (or younger) sister of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}}. === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Waniguchi gong (craft) - late Muromachi period * Image of Komori Kuroda Daimyōjin honji (painting) * Ninja sword (craft) - early Edo period * Bamboo spear (craft) - early Edo period === Transportation === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kisogawa Station|Q871019|WD=Q871019}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152}}. *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kuroda Station (Aichi)|Q6446282|WD=Q6446282|lt=Kuroda Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850}}. *1 minute on foot from “Matsuyama-higashi” stop on the Kisogawa/Kitagata route of {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|WD=Q11223722}}. *5 minutes on foot from “{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Municipal Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital|Q11352566|WD=Q11352566|lt=Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital}}” stop on the same i-Bus route. === Mikago Festival === A ritual held on lunar August 15. Images of Princes Oke and Woke are placed in {{ill|lt=kago|Q936754|WD=Q936754}} palanquins, taro is offered, and prayers are made for warding off misfortune and abundant harvest. This derives from the following tradition. *{{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842}}, father of Oke and Woke, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Richū|Q329704|WD=Q329704}}. {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010}} (his nephew) intended to pass the throne to him, but in Ankō 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432}}), Ankō was assassinated by {{ill|lt=Mayowa no Ōkimi|Q11582590|WD=Q11582590}}. Prince Ōhatsuse (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}}), who had long resented this, lured Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko to hunting grounds at Kayano in {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}} and shot him. *The brothers Oke (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninken|Q357266|WD=Q357266}}) and Woke (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Kenzō|Q360966|WD=Q360966}}), fearing further danger, fled the capital. On their way toward {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916}} in Owari, they passed through Mino and reached Kuroda Shrine. *They stopped their palanquin in the forest of Kuroda Shrine and spent the night inside. It was the full-moon night of lunar August 15. Villagers treated them kindly with dishes of {{ill|lt=taro|Q227997|WD=Q227997}}. In thanks, the princes offered dew from taro leaves to Kuroda Shrine, prayed for villagers’ happiness, and departed before dawn. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こもりかつてしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建 = Unknown | 別名 = Hakusan-sha
Kuroda Daimyōjin | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Komori
Katte Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Komori Katte Shrine''' (こもりかってじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kuroda, Kisogawa-chō|Q17210627|WD=Q17210627|1=Kisogawa-chō Kuroda}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with “Kuroda Shrine” in Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Overview === *The founding period is unknown. Since {{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842|1=Ichinobe no Oshiwa}}, father of Princes Oke and Woke, is said to have been killed by Emperor Yūryaku in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}), it is presumed this shrine had already been founded before then. Kuroda Shrine was the formal name, but it was also called Komori Katte Shrine at that time. *It was long regarded as an important shrine. There are records of imperial offerings in the reigns of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku|1=Empress Kōken}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}}, and in {{ill|lt=951|Q23654|WD=Q23654|1=951}} ({{ill|lt=Tenryaku|Q1346281|WD=Q1346281|1=Tenryaku}} 5), an envoy of {{ill|lt=Emperor Murakami|Q374015|WD=Q374015|1=Emperor Murakami}} prayed there for recovery from illness. *In {{ill|lt=1582|Q6719|WD=Q6719|1=1582}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 10), it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Sawai Katsushige|Q11553123|WD=Q11553123|1=Sawai Katsushige}}, lord of {{ill|lt=Kuroda Castle (Owari Province)|Q11678587|WD=Q11678587|lt=Kuroda Castle|1=Kuroda Castle}} and chief retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobukatsu|Q3044815|WD=Q3044815|1=Oda Nobukatsu}}. *In the Edo period it was called “Kuroda Daimyōjin” and “Hakusan Shrine.” When it became gōsha for the northwestern Haguri District area in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was renamed Komori Katte Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Haraedo no Kami}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yodobime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kokūtsuhime-no-Mikoto) *:Child of {{ill|lt=Okinaga Sukune no Ō|Q11492327|WD=Q11492327|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko}} and elder (or younger) sister of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Waniguchi gong (craft) - late Muromachi period * Image of Komori Kuroda Daimyōjin honji (painting) * Ninja sword (craft) - early Edo period * Bamboo spear (craft) - early Edo period === Transportation === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kisogawa Station|Q871019|WD=Q871019|1=Kisogawa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}}. *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kuroda Station (Aichi)|Q6446282|WD=Q6446282|lt=Kuroda Station|1=Kuroda Station (Aichi)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}. *1 minute on foot from “Matsuyama-higashi” stop on the Kisogawa/Kitagata route of {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|WD=Q11223722|1=I-Bus}}. *5 minutes on foot from “{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Municipal Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital|Q11352566|WD=Q11352566|lt=Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital|1=Kisogawa Hospital}}” stop on the same i-Bus route. === Mikago Festival === A ritual held on lunar August 15. Images of Princes Oke and Woke are placed in {{ill|lt=kago|Q936754|WD=Q936754|1=Kago}} palanquins, taro is offered, and prayers are made for warding off misfortune and abundant harvest. This derives from the following tradition. *{{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842|1=Ichinobe no Oshiwa}}, father of Oke and Woke, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Richū|Q329704|WD=Q329704|1=Emperor Richū}}. {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} (his nephew) intended to pass the throne to him, but in Ankō 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}), Ankō was assassinated by {{ill|lt=Mayowa no Ōkimi|Q11582590|WD=Q11582590|1=Prince Mayowa}}. Prince Ōhatsuse (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}), who had long resented this, lured Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko to hunting grounds at Kayano in {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} and shot him. *The brothers Oke (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninken|Q357266|WD=Q357266|1=Emperor Ninken}}) and Woke (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Kenzō|Q360966|WD=Q360966|1=Emperor Kenzō}}), fearing further danger, fled the capital. On their way toward {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}} in Owari, they passed through Mino and reached Kuroda Shrine. *They stopped their palanquin in the forest of Kuroda Shrine and spent the night inside. It was the full-moon night of lunar August 15. Villagers treated them kindly with dishes of {{ill|lt=taro|Q227997|WD=Q227997|1=Taro}}. In thanks, the princes offered dew from taro leaves to Kuroda Shrine, prayed for villagers’ happiness, and departed before dawn. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こもりかつてしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建 = Unknown | 別名 = Hakusan-sha
Kuroda Daimyōjin | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Komori
Katte Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Komori Katte Shrine''' (こもりかってじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kuroda, Kisogawa-chō|Q17210627|WD=Q17210627|1=Kisogawa-chō Kuroda}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with “Kuroda Shrine” in Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Overview === *The founding period is unknown. Since {{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842|1=Ichinobe no Oshiwa}}, father of Princes Oke and Woke, is said to have been killed by Emperor Yūryaku in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}), it is presumed this shrine had already been founded before then. Kuroda Shrine was the formal name, but it was also called Komori Katte Shrine at that time. *It was long regarded as an important shrine. There are records of imperial offerings in the reigns of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku|1=Empress Kōken}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}}, and in {{ill|lt=951|Q23654|WD=Q23654|1=951}} ({{ill|lt=Tenryaku|Q1346281|WD=Q1346281|1=Tenryaku}} 5), an envoy of {{ill|lt=Emperor Murakami|Q374015|WD=Q374015|1=Emperor Murakami}} prayed there for recovery from illness. *In {{ill|lt=1582|Q6719|WD=Q6719|1=1582}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 10), it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Sawai Katsushige|Q11553123|WD=Q11553123|1=Sawai Katsushige}}, lord of {{ill|lt=Kuroda Castle (Owari Province)|Q11678587|WD=Q11678587|lt=Kuroda Castle|1=Kuroda Castle}} and chief retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobukatsu|Q3044815|WD=Q3044815|1=Oda Nobukatsu}}. *In the Edo period it was called “Kuroda Daimyōjin” and “Hakusan Shrine.” When it became gōsha for the northwestern Haguri District area in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was renamed Komori Katte Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Haraedo no Kami}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yodobime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kokūtsuhime-no-Mikoto) *:Child of {{ill|lt=Okinaga Sukune no Ō|Q11492327|WD=Q11492327|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko}} and elder (or younger) sister of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Waniguchi gong (craft) - late Muromachi period * Image of Komori Kuroda Daimyōjin honji (painting) * Ninja sword (craft) - early Edo period * Bamboo spear (craft) - early Edo period === Transportation === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kisogawa Station|Q871019|WD=Q871019|1=Kisogawa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}}. *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kuroda Station (Aichi)|Q6446282|WD=Q6446282|lt=Kuroda Station|1=Kuroda Station (Aichi)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}. *1 minute on foot from “Matsuyama-higashi” stop on the Kisogawa/Kitagata route of {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|WD=Q11223722|1=I-Bus}}. *5 minutes on foot from “{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Municipal Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital|Q11352566|WD=Q11352566|lt=Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital|1=Kisogawa Hospital}}” stop on the same i-Bus route. === Mikago Festival === A ritual held on lunar August 15. Images of Princes Oke and Woke are placed in {{ill|lt=kago|Q936754|WD=Q936754|1=Kago}} palanquins, taro is offered, and prayers are made for warding off misfortune and abundant harvest. This derives from the following tradition. *{{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842|1=Ichinobe no Oshiwa}}, father of Oke and Woke, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Richū|Q329704|WD=Q329704|1=Emperor Richū}}. {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} (his nephew) intended to pass the throne to him, but in Ankō 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}), Ankō was assassinated by {{ill|lt=Mayowa no Ōkimi|Q11582590|WD=Q11582590|1=Prince Mayowa}}. Prince Ōhatsuse (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}), who had long resented this, lured Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko to hunting grounds at Kayano in {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} and shot him. *The brothers Oke (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninken|Q357266|WD=Q357266|1=Emperor Ninken}}) and Woke (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Kenzō|Q360966|WD=Q360966|1=Emperor Kenzō}}), fearing further danger, fled the capital. On their way toward {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}} in Owari, they passed through Mino and reached Kuroda Shrine. *They stopped their palanquin in the forest of Kuroda Shrine and spent the night inside. It was the full-moon night of lunar August 15. Villagers treated them kindly with dishes of {{ill|lt=taro|Q227997|WD=Q227997|1=Taro}}. In thanks, the princes offered dew from taro leaves to Kuroda Shrine, prayed for villagers’ happiness, and departed before dawn. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こもりかつてしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Kuroda Shrine was the formal name, but it was also called Komori Katte Shrine at that time. *It was long regarded as an important shrine. There are records of imperial offerings in the reigns of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku|1=Empress Kōken}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}}, and in {{ill|lt=951|Q23654|WD=Q23654|1=951}} ({{ill|lt=Tenryaku|Q1346281|WD=Q1346281|1=Tenryaku}} 5), an envoy of {{ill|lt=Emperor Murakami|Q374015|WD=Q374015|1=Emperor Murakami}} prayed there for recovery from illness. *In {{ill|lt=1582|Q6719|WD=Q6719|1=1582}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 10), it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Sawai Katsushige|Q11553123|WD=Q11553123|1=Sawai Katsushige}}, lord of {{ill|lt=Kuroda Castle (Owari Province)|Q11678587|WD=Q11678587|lt=Kuroda Castle|1=Kuroda Castle}} and chief retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobukatsu|Q3044815|WD=Q3044815|1=Oda Nobukatsu}}. *In the Edo period it was called “Kuroda Daimyōjin” and “Hakusan Shrine.” When it became gōsha for the northwestern Haguri District area in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was renamed Komori Katte Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Haraedo no Kami}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yodobime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kokūtsuhime-no-Mikoto) *:Child of {{ill|lt=Okinaga Sukune no Ō|Q11492327|WD=Q11492327|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko}} and elder (or younger) sister of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Waniguchi gong (craft) - late Muromachi period * Image of Komori Kuroda Daimyōjin honji (painting) * Ninja sword (craft) - early Edo period * Bamboo spear (craft) - early Edo period === Transportation === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kisogawa Station|Q871019|WD=Q871019|1=Kisogawa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}}. *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kuroda Station (Aichi)|Q6446282|WD=Q6446282|lt=Kuroda Station|1=Kuroda Station (Aichi)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}. *1 minute on foot from “Matsuyama-higashi” stop on the Kisogawa/Kitagata route of {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|WD=Q11223722|1=I-Bus}}. *5 minutes on foot from “{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Municipal Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital|Q11352566|WD=Q11352566|lt=Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital|1=Kisogawa Hospital}}” stop on the same i-Bus route. === Mikago Festival === A ritual held on lunar August 15. Images of Princes Oke and Woke are placed in {{ill|lt=kago|Q936754|WD=Q936754|1=Kago}} palanquins, taro is offered, and prayers are made for warding off misfortune and abundant harvest. This derives from the following tradition. *{{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842|1=Ichinobe no Oshiwa}}, father of Oke and Woke, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Richū|Q329704|WD=Q329704|1=Emperor Richū}}. {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} (his nephew) intended to pass the throne to him, but in Ankō 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}), Ankō was assassinated by {{ill|lt=Mayowa no Ōkimi|Q11582590|WD=Q11582590|1=Prince Mayowa}}. Prince Ōhatsuse (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}), who had long resented this, lured Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko to hunting grounds at Kayano in {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} and shot him. *The brothers Oke (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninken|Q357266|WD=Q357266|1=Emperor Ninken}}) and Woke (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Kenzō|Q360966|WD=Q360966|1=Emperor Kenzō}}), fearing further danger, fled the capital. On their way toward {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}} in Owari, they passed through Mino and reached Kuroda Shrine. *They stopped their palanquin in the forest of Kuroda Shrine and spent the night inside. It was the full-moon night of lunar August 15. Villagers treated them kindly with dishes of {{ill|lt=taro|Q227997|WD=Q227997|1=Taro}}. In thanks, the princes offered dew from taro leaves to Kuroda Shrine, prayed for villagers’ happiness, and departed before dawn. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こもりかつてしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]]

(UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称 = Komori Katte Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Komorikatte shrine1.jpg|250px]]

Katte Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Komori Katte Shrine''' (こもりかってじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kuroda, Kisogawa-chō|Q17210627|WD=Q17210627|1=Kisogawa-chō Kuroda}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with “Kuroda Shrine” in Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Overview === *The founding period is unknown. Since {{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842|1=Ichinobe no Oshiwa}}, father of Princes Oke and Woke, is said to have been killed by Emperor Yūryaku in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}), it is presumed this shrine had already been founded before then. Kuroda Shrine was the formal name, but it was also called Komori Katte Shrine at that time. *It was long regarded as an important shrine. There are records of imperial offerings in the reigns of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku|1=Empress Kōken}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}}, and in {{ill|lt=951|Q23654|WD=Q23654|1=951}} ({{ill|lt=Tenryaku|Q1346281|WD=Q1346281|1=Tenryaku}} 5), an envoy of {{ill|lt=Emperor Murakami|Q374015|WD=Q374015|1=Emperor Murakami}} prayed there for recovery from illness. *In {{ill|lt=1582|Q6719|WD=Q6719|1=1582}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 10), it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Sawai Katsushige|Q11553123|WD=Q11553123|1=Sawai Katsushige}}, lord of {{ill|lt=Kuroda Castle (Owari Province)|Q11678587|WD=Q11678587|lt=Kuroda Castle|1=Kuroda Castle}} and chief retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobukatsu|Q3044815|WD=Q3044815|1=Oda Nobukatsu}}. *In the Edo period it was called “Kuroda Daimyōjin” and “Hakusan Shrine.” When it became gōsha for the northwestern Haguri District area in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was renamed Komori Katte Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Haraedo no Kami}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yodobime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kokūtsuhime-no-Mikoto) *:Child of {{ill|lt=Okinaga Sukune no Ō|Q11492327|WD=Q11492327|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko}} and elder (or younger) sister of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Waniguchi gong (craft) - late Muromachi period * Image of Komori Kuroda Daimyōjin honji (painting) * Ninja sword (craft) - early Edo period * Bamboo spear (craft) - early Edo period === Transportation === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kisogawa Station|Q871019|WD=Q871019|1=Kisogawa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}}. *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kuroda Station (Aichi)|Q6446282|WD=Q6446282|lt=Kuroda Station|1=Kuroda Station (Aichi)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}. *1 minute on foot from “Matsuyama-higashi” stop on the Kisogawa/Kitagata route of {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|WD=Q11223722|1=I-Bus}}. *5 minutes on foot from “{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Municipal Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital|Q11352566|WD=Q11352566|lt=Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital|1=Kisogawa Hospital}}” stop on the same i-Bus route. === Mikago Festival === A ritual held on lunar August 15. Images of Princes Oke and Woke are placed in {{ill|lt=kago|Q936754|WD=Q936754|1=Kago}} palanquins, taro is offered, and prayers are made for warding off misfortune and abundant harvest. This derives from the following tradition. *{{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842|1=Ichinobe no Oshiwa}}, father of Oke and Woke, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Richū|Q329704|WD=Q329704|1=Emperor Richū}}. {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} (his nephew) intended to pass the throne to him, but in Ankō 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}), Ankō was assassinated by {{ill|lt=Mayowa no Ōkimi|Q11582590|WD=Q11582590|1=Prince Mayowa}}. On their way toward {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}} in Owari, they passed through Mino and reached Kuroda Shrine. *They stopped their palanquin in the forest of Kuroda Shrine and spent the night inside. It was the full-moon night of lunar August 15. Villagers treated them kindly with dishes of {{ill|lt=taro|Q227997|WD=Q227997|1=Taro}}. In thanks, the princes offered dew from taro leaves to Kuroda Shrine, prayed for villagers’ happiness, and departed before dawn. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]]

8180005010291 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市木曽川町黒田往還東東ノ切11', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:籠守勝手神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11603933}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 11:44 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称 = Komori Katte Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Komorikatte shrine1.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Kuroda, Kisogawa-chō|Q17210627|WD=Q17210627|1=Kisogawa-chō Kuroda}} Ōkanhigashi Higashinokiri 11 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Haraedo no Kami}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yodobime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建 = Unknown | 別名 = Hakusan-sha
Kuroda Daimyōjin | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Komori
Katte Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Komori Katte Shrine''' (こもりかってじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kuroda, Kisogawa-chō|Q17210627|WD=Q17210627|1=Kisogawa-chō Kuroda}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with “Kuroda Shrine” in Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Overview === *The founding period is unknown. Since {{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842|1=Ichinobe no Oshiwa}}, father of Princes Oke and Woke, is said to have been killed by Emperor Yūryaku in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}), it is presumed this shrine had already been founded before then. Kuroda Shrine was the formal name, but it was also called Komori Katte Shrine at that time. *It was long regarded as an important shrine. There are records of imperial offerings in the reigns of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku|1=Empress Kōken}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}}, and in {{ill|lt=951|Q23654|WD=Q23654|1=951}} ({{ill|lt=Tenryaku|Q1346281|WD=Q1346281|1=Tenryaku}} 5), an envoy of {{ill|lt=Emperor Murakami|Q374015|WD=Q374015|1=Emperor Murakami}} prayed there for recovery from illness. *In {{ill|lt=1582|Q6719|WD=Q6719|1=1582}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 10), it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Sawai Katsushige|Q11553123|WD=Q11553123|1=Sawai Katsushige}}, lord of {{ill|lt=Kuroda Castle (Owari Province)|Q11678587|WD=Q11678587|lt=Kuroda Castle|1=Kuroda Castle}} and chief retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobukatsu|Q3044815|WD=Q3044815|1=Oda Nobukatsu}}. *In the Edo period it was called “Kuroda Daimyōjin” and “Hakusan Shrine.” When it became gōsha for the northwestern Haguri District area in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was renamed Komori Katte Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Haraedo no Kami}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yodobime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kokūtsuhime-no-Mikoto) *:Child of {{ill|lt=Okinaga Sukune no Ō|Q11492327|WD=Q11492327|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko}} and elder (or younger) sister of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Waniguchi gong (craft) - late Muromachi period * Image of Komori Kuroda Daimyōjin honji (painting) * Ninja sword (craft) - early Edo period * Bamboo spear (craft) - early Edo period === Transportation === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kisogawa Station|Q871019|WD=Q871019|1=Kisogawa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}}. *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kuroda Station (Aichi)|Q6446282|WD=Q6446282|lt=Kuroda Station|1=Kuroda Station (Aichi)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}. *1 minute on foot from “Matsuyama-higashi” stop on the Kisogawa/Kitagata route of {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|WD=Q11223722|1=I-Bus}}. *5 minutes on foot from “{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Municipal Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital|Q11352566|WD=Q11352566|lt=Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital|1=Kisogawa Hospital}}” stop on the same i-Bus route. === Mikago Festival === A ritual held on lunar August 15. Images of Princes Oke and Woke are placed in {{ill|lt=kago|Q936754|WD=Q936754|1=Kago}} palanquins, taro is offered, and prayers are made for warding off misfortune and abundant harvest. This derives from the following tradition. *{{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842|1=Ichinobe no Oshiwa}}, father of Oke and Woke, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Richū|Q329704|WD=Q329704|1=Emperor Richū}}. {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} (his nephew) intended to pass the throne to him, but in Ankō 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}), Ankō was assassinated by {{ill|lt=Mayowa no Ōkimi|Q11582590|WD=Q11582590|1=Prince Mayowa}}. Prince Ōhatsuse (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}), who had long resented this, lured Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko to hunting grounds at Kayano in {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} and shot him. *The brothers Oke (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninken|Q357266|WD=Q357266|1=Emperor Ninken}}) and Woke (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Kenzō|Q360966|WD=Q360966|1=Emperor Kenzō}}), fearing further danger, fled the capital. On their way toward {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}} in Owari, they passed through Mino and reached Kuroda Shrine. *They stopped their palanquin in the forest of Kuroda Shrine and spent the night inside. It was the full-moon night of lunar August 15. Villagers treated them kindly with dishes of {{ill|lt=taro|Q227997|WD=Q227997|1=Taro}}. In thanks, the princes offered dew from taro leaves to Kuroda Shrine, prayed for villagers’ happiness, and departed before dawn. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]]

Since {{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842|1=Ichinobe no Oshiwa}}, father of Princes Oke and Woke, is said to have been killed by Emperor Yūryaku in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}), it is presumed this shrine had already been founded before then. Kuroda Shrine was the formal name, but it was also called Komori Katte Shrine at that time. *It was long regarded as an important shrine. There are records of imperial offerings in the reigns of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku|1=Empress Kōken}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}}, and in {{ill|lt=951|Q23654|WD=Q23654|1=951}} ({{ill|lt=Tenryaku|Q1346281|WD=Q1346281|1=Tenryaku}} 5), an envoy of {{ill|lt=Emperor Murakami|Q374015|WD=Q374015|1=Emperor Murakami}} prayed there for recovery from illness. *In {{ill|lt=1582|Q6719|WD=Q6719|1=1582}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 10), it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Sawai Katsushige|Q11553123|WD=Q11553123|1=Sawai Katsushige}}, lord of {{ill|lt=Kuroda Castle (Owari Province)|Q11678587|WD=Q11678587|lt=Kuroda Castle|1=Kuroda Castle}} and chief retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobukatsu|Q3044815|WD=Q3044815|1=Oda Nobukatsu}}. *In the Edo period it was called “Kuroda Daimyōjin” and “Hakusan Shrine.” When it became gōsha for the northwestern Haguri District area in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was renamed Komori Katte Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Haraedo no Kami}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yodobime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kokūtsuhime-no-Mikoto) *:Child of {{ill|lt=Okinaga Sukune no Ō|Q11492327|WD=Q11492327|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko}} and elder (or younger) sister of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Waniguchi gong (craft) - late Muromachi period * Image of Komori Kuroda Daimyōjin honji (painting) * Ninja sword (craft) - early Edo period * Bamboo spear (craft) - early Edo period === Transportation === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kisogawa Station|Q871019|WD=Q871019|1=Kisogawa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}}. *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kuroda Station (Aichi)|Q6446282|WD=Q6446282|lt=Kuroda Station|1=Kuroda Station (Aichi)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}. *1 minute on foot from “Matsuyama-higashi” stop on the Kisogawa/Kitagata route of {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|WD=Q11223722|1=I-Bus}}. *5 minutes on foot from “{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Municipal Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital|Q11352566|WD=Q11352566|lt=Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital|1=Kisogawa Hospital}}” stop on the same i-Bus route. === Mikago Festival === A ritual held on lunar August 15. Images of Princes Oke and Woke are placed in {{ill|lt=kago|Q936754|WD=Q936754|1=Kago}} palanquins, taro is offered, and prayers are made for warding off misfortune and abundant harvest. This derives from the following tradition. *{{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842|1=Ichinobe no Oshiwa}}, father of Oke and Woke, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Richū|Q329704|WD=Q329704|1=Emperor Richū}}. {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} (his nephew) intended to pass the throne to him, but in Ankō 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}), Ankō was assassinated by {{ill|lt=Mayowa no Ōkimi|Q11582590|WD=Q11582590|1=Prince Mayowa}}. Prince Ōhatsuse (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}), who had long resented this, lured Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko to hunting grounds at Kayano in {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} and shot him. *The brothers Oke (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninken|Q357266|WD=Q357266|1=Emperor Ninken}}) and Woke (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Kenzō|Q360966|WD=Q360966|1=Emperor Kenzō}}), fearing further danger, fled the capital. On their way toward {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}} in Owari, they passed through Mino and reached Kuroda Shrine. *They stopped their palanquin in the forest of Kuroda Shrine and spent the night inside. It was the full-moon night of lunar August 15. Villagers treated them kindly with dishes of {{ill|lt=taro|Q227997|WD=Q227997|1=Taro}}. In thanks, the princes offered dew from taro leaves to Kuroda Shrine, prayed for villagers’ happiness, and departed before dawn. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]]

Katte Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Komori Katte Shrine''' (こもりかってじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kuroda, Kisogawa-chō|Q17210627|WD=Q17210627|1=Kisogawa-chō Kuroda}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with “Kuroda Shrine” in Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Overview === *The founding period is unknown. Since {{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842|1=Ichinobe no Oshiwa}}, father of Princes Oke and Woke, is said to have been killed by Emperor Yūryaku in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}), it is presumed this shrine had already been founded before then. Kuroda Shrine was the formal name, but it was also called Komori Katte Shrine at that time. *It was long regarded as an important shrine. There are records of imperial offerings in the reigns of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku|1=Empress Kōken}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}}, and in {{ill|lt=951|Q23654|WD=Q23654|1=951}} ({{ill|lt=Tenryaku|Q1346281|WD=Q1346281|1=Tenryaku}} 5), an envoy of {{ill|lt=Emperor Murakami|Q374015|WD=Q374015|1=Emperor Murakami}} prayed there for recovery from illness. *In {{ill|lt=1582|Q6719|WD=Q6719|1=1582}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 10), it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Sawai Katsushige|Q11553123|WD=Q11553123|1=Sawai Katsushige}}, lord of {{ill|lt=Kuroda Castle (Owari Province)|Q11678587|WD=Q11678587|lt=Kuroda Castle|1=Kuroda Castle}} and chief retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobukatsu|Q3044815|WD=Q3044815|1=Oda Nobukatsu}}. *In the Edo period it was called “Kuroda Daimyōjin” and “Hakusan Shrine.” When it became gōsha for the northwestern Haguri District area in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was renamed Komori Katte Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Haraedo no Kami}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yodobime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kokūtsuhime-no-Mikoto) *:Child of {{ill|lt=Okinaga Sukune no Ō|Q11492327|WD=Q11492327|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko}} and elder (or younger) sister of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Waniguchi gong (craft) - Images of Princes Oke and Woke are placed in {{ill|lt=kago|Q936754|WD=Q936754|1=Kago}} palanquins, taro is offered, and prayers are made for warding off misfortune and abundant harvest. This derives from the following tradition. *{{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842|1=Ichinobe no Oshiwa}}, father of Oke and Woke, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Richū|Q329704|WD=Q329704|1=Emperor Richū}}. {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} (his nephew) intended to pass the throne to him, but in Ankō 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}), Ankō was assassinated by {{ill|lt=Mayowa no Ōkimi|Q11582590|WD=Q11582590|1=Prince Mayowa}}. former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建 = Unknown | 別名 = Hakusan-sha
Kuroda Daimyōjin | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Komori
Katte Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Komori Katte Shrine''' (こもりかってじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kuroda, Kisogawa-chō|Q17210627|WD=Q17210627|1=Kisogawa-chō Kuroda}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with “Kuroda Shrine” in Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Overview === *The founding period is unknown. Since {{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842|1=Ichinobe no Oshiwa}}, father of Princes Oke and Woke, is said to have been killed by Emperor Yūryaku in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}), it is presumed this shrine had already been founded before then. Kuroda Shrine was the formal name, but it was also called Komori Katte Shrine at that time. *It was long regarded as an important shrine. There are records of imperial offerings in the reigns of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku|1=Empress Kōken}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}}, and in {{ill|lt=951|Q23654|WD=Q23654|1=951}} ({{ill|lt=Tenryaku|Q1346281|WD=Q1346281|1=Tenryaku}} 5), an envoy of {{ill|lt=Emperor Murakami|Q374015|WD=Q374015|1=Emperor Murakami}} prayed there for recovery from illness. *In {{ill|lt=1582|Q6719|WD=Q6719|1=1582}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 10), it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Sawai Katsushige|Q11553123|WD=Q11553123|1=Sawai Katsushige}}, lord of {{ill|lt=Kuroda Castle (Owari Province)|Q11678587|WD=Q11678587|lt=Kuroda Castle|1=Kuroda Castle}} and chief retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobukatsu|Q3044815|WD=Q3044815|1=Oda Nobukatsu}}. *In the Edo period it was called “Kuroda Daimyōjin” and “Hakusan Shrine.” When it became gōsha for the northwestern Haguri District area in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was renamed Komori Katte Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Haraedo no Kami}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yodobime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kokūtsuhime-no-Mikoto) *:Child of {{ill|lt=Okinaga Sukune no Ō|Q11492327|WD=Q11492327|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko}} and elder (or younger) sister of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Waniguchi gong (craft) - late Muromachi period * Image of Komori Kuroda Daimyōjin honji (painting) * Ninja sword (craft) - early Edo period * Bamboo spear (craft) - early Edo period === Transportation === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kisogawa Station|Q871019|WD=Q871019|1=Kisogawa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}}. *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kuroda Station (Aichi)|Q6446282|WD=Q6446282|lt=Kuroda Station|1=Kuroda Station (Aichi)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}. *1 minute on foot from “Matsuyama-higashi” stop on the Kisogawa/Kitagata route of {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|WD=Q11223722|1=I-Bus}}. *5 minutes on foot from “{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Municipal Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital|Q11352566|WD=Q11352566|lt=Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital|1=Kisogawa Hospital}}” stop on the same i-Bus route. === Mikago Festival === A ritual held on lunar August 15. Images of Princes Oke and Woke are placed in {{ill|lt=kago|Q936754|WD=Q936754|1=Kago}} palanquins, taro is offered, and prayers are made for warding off misfortune and abundant harvest. This derives from the following tradition. *{{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842|1=Ichinobe no Oshiwa}}, father of Oke and Woke, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Richū|Q329704|WD=Q329704|1=Emperor Richū}}. {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} (his nephew) intended to pass the throne to him, but in Ankō 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}), Ankō was assassinated by {{ill|lt=Mayowa no Ōkimi|Q11582590|WD=Q11582590|1=Prince Mayowa}}. Prince Ōhatsuse (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}), who had long resented this, lured Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko to hunting grounds at Kayano in {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} and shot him. *The brothers Oke (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninken|Q357266|WD=Q357266|1=Emperor Ninken}}) and Woke (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Kenzō|Q360966|WD=Q360966|1=Emperor Kenzō}}), fearing further danger, fled the capital. On their way toward {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}} in Owari, they passed through Mino and reached Kuroda Shrine. *They stopped their palanquin in the forest of Kuroda Shrine and spent the night inside. It was the full-moon night of lunar August 15. Villagers treated them kindly with dishes of {{ill|lt=taro|Q227997|WD=Q227997|1=Taro}}. In thanks, the princes offered dew from taro leaves to Kuroda Shrine, prayed for villagers’ happiness, and departed before dawn. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]]

Since {{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842|1=Ichinobe no Oshiwa}}, father of Princes Oke and Woke, is said to have been killed by Emperor Yūryaku in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}), it is presumed this shrine had already been founded before then. Kuroda Shrine was the formal name, but it was also called Komori Katte Shrine at that time. *It was long regarded as an important shrine. There are records of imperial offerings in the reigns of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku|1=Empress Kōken}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}}, and in {{ill|lt=951|Q23654|WD=Q23654|1=951}} ({{ill|lt=Tenryaku|Q1346281|WD=Q1346281|1=Tenryaku}} 5), an envoy of {{ill|lt=Emperor Murakami|Q374015|WD=Q374015|1=Emperor Murakami}} prayed there for recovery from illness. *In {{ill|lt=1582|Q6719|WD=Q6719|1=1582}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 10), it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Sawai Katsushige|Q11553123|WD=Q11553123|1=Sawai Katsushige}}, lord of {{ill|lt=Kuroda Castle (Owari Province)|Q11678587|WD=Q11678587|lt=Kuroda Castle|1=Kuroda Castle}} and chief retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobukatsu|Q3044815|WD=Q3044815|1=Oda Nobukatsu}}. *In the Edo period it was called “Kuroda Daimyōjin” and “Hakusan Shrine.” When it became gōsha for the northwestern Haguri District area in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was renamed Komori Katte Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Haraedo no Kami}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yodobime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kokūtsuhime-no-Mikoto) *:Child of {{ill|lt=Okinaga Sukune no Ō|Q11492327|WD=Q11492327|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko}} and elder (or younger) sister of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Waniguchi gong (craft) - late Muromachi period * Image of Komori Kuroda Daimyōjin honji (painting) * Ninja sword (craft) - early Edo period * Bamboo spear (craft) - early Edo period === Transportation === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kisogawa Station|Q871019|WD=Q871019|1=Kisogawa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}}. *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kuroda Station (Aichi)|Q6446282|WD=Q6446282|lt=Kuroda Station|1=Kuroda Station (Aichi)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}. *1 minute on foot from “Matsuyama-higashi” stop on the Kisogawa/Kitagata route of {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|WD=Q11223722|1=I-Bus}}. *5 minutes on foot from “{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Municipal Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital|Q11352566|WD=Q11352566|lt=Kisogawa Citizen’s Hospital|1=Kisogawa Hospital}}” stop on the same i-Bus route. === Mikago Festival === A ritual held on lunar August 15. Images of Princes Oke and Woke are placed in {{ill|lt=kago|Q936754|WD=Q936754|1=Kago}} palanquins, taro is offered, and prayers are made for warding off misfortune and abundant harvest. This derives from the following tradition. *{{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|WD=Q2297842|1=Ichinobe no Oshiwa}}, father of Oke and Woke, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Richū|Q329704|WD=Q329704|1=Emperor Richū}}. {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} (his nephew) intended to pass the throne to him, but in Ankō 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}), Ankō was assassinated by {{ill|lt=Mayowa no Ōkimi|Q11582590|WD=Q11582590|1=Prince Mayowa}}. Prince Ōhatsuse (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}), who had long resented this, lured Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko to hunting grounds at Kayano in {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} and shot him. *The brothers Oke (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninken|Q357266|WD=Q357266|1=Emperor Ninken}}) and Woke (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Kenzō|Q360966|WD=Q360966|1=Emperor Kenzō}}), fearing further danger, fled the capital. On their way toward {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}} in Owari, they passed through Mino and reached Kuroda Shrine. *They stopped their palanquin in the forest of Kuroda Shrine and spent the night inside. It was the full-moon night of lunar August 15. Villagers treated them kindly with dishes of {{ill|lt=taro|Q227997|WD=Q227997|1=Taro}}. In thanks, the princes offered dew from taro leaves to Kuroda Shrine, prayed for villagers’ happiness, and departed before dawn. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komori Katte Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]]

Katte Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Komori Katte Shrine''' (こもりかってじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kuroda, Kisogawa-chō|Q17210627|qid=Q17210627|1=Kisogawa-chō Kuroda}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|qid=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with “Kuroda Shrine” in Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Overview === *The founding period is unknown. Since {{ill|lt=Ichinobe Oshiwa no Miko|Q2297842|qid=Q2297842|1=Ichinobe no Oshiwa}}, father of Princes Oke and Woke, is said to have been killed by Emperor Yūryaku in {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|qid=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=456|Q30432|qid=Q30432|1=456}}), it is presumed this shrine had already been founded before then. Kuroda Shrine was the formal name, but it was also called Komori Katte Shrine at that time. *It was long regarded as an important shrine. There are records of imperial offerings in the reigns of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōken|Q232955|qid=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku|1=Empress Kōken}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|qid=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}}, and in {{ill|lt=951|Q23654|qid=Q23654|1=951}} ({{ill|lt=Tenryaku|Q1346281|qid=Q1346281|1=Tenryaku}} 5), an envoy of {{ill|lt=Emperor Murakami|Q374015|qid=Q374015|1=Emperor Murakami}} prayed there for recovery from illness. *In {{ill|lt=1582|Q6719|qid=Q6719|1=1582}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 10), it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Sawai Katsushige|Q11553123|qid=Q11553123|1=Sawai Katsushige}}, lord of {{ill|lt=Kuroda Castle (Owari Province)|Q11678587|qid=Q11678587|lt=Kuroda Castle|1=Kuroda Castle}} and chief retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobukatsu|Q3044815|qid=Q3044815|1=Oda Nobukatsu}}. *In the Edo period it was called “Kuroda Daimyōjin” and “Hakusan Shrine.” When it became gōsha for the northwestern Haguri District area in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was renamed Komori Katte Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|Q11588504|qid=Q11588504|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Haraedo no Kami}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yodobime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Kokūtsuhime-no-Mikoto) *:Child of {{ill|lt=Okinaga Sukune no Ō|Q11492327|qid=Q11492327|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko}} and elder (or younger) sister of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Waniguchi gong (craft) -
